steps.name: Domain types and multi-session store
steps.description: Add new algebraic types (AggregateMetrics, SessionSummary, TimeWindowConfig, PMViewMode, CompactionEstimate, AgentMetrics, SessionDetailData) to types.ts. Create multiSessionStore adapter to track SessionMetrics for all active sessions. Create sessionDiscovery adapter to poll sessions table.
criteria: Empty aggregate yields zero totals and empty session list
Single session aggregate equals that session's metrics
Multi-session store tracks add/remove of sessions independently

tests: tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/cross-session-aggregation.test.ts#Empty aggregate when no sessions are active
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/cross-session-aggregation.test.ts#Single session aggregate equals that session's metrics

steps.name: View registration and backward compatibility
steps.description: Register Performance Monitor as a new mode view alongside Oscilloscope and Dashboard. Wire multi-session store initialization in plugin onLoad. Verify existing oscilloscope data pipeline unchanged.
criteria: Performance Monitor registered as a mode view in Usage tab
Existing oscilloscope buffer operations produce unchanged results
Existing view registrations preserved, floating panels unbroken
Shared time-series buffer identical when read from PM or oscilloscope

tests: tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/backward-compatibility.test.ts#Existing time-series buffer operations produce unchanged results
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/backward-compatibility.test.ts#Oscilloscope waveform data is identical when accessed from PM context
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/backward-compatibility.test.ts#Token rate from shared buffer matches between oscilloscope and PM
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/backward-compatibility.test.ts#Data pipeline remains valid across mode switches

steps.name: Cross-session aggregator pure functions
steps.description: Create crossSessionAggregator.ts: pure function mapping Array<SessionMetrics> to AggregateMetrics. Sum token rates, cost rates, agent counts. Sort sessions by rate descending. Create urgencyThresholds.ts with shared threshold constants.
criteria: Aggregate token rate equals sum of per-session burn rates
Aggregate active agents equals sum of per-session agent counts
Per-session breakdown sorted by token rate descending
Aggregate updates correctly when sessions added or removed
Sum invariant holds for any combination of session metrics

tests: tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/cross-session-aggregation.test.ts#User views aggregate resource consumption across all active sessions
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/cross-session-aggregation.test.ts#Aggregate token rate is the sum of per-session burn rates
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/cross-session-aggregation.test.ts#Aggregate active agents is the sum of per-session agent counts
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/cross-session-aggregation.test.ts#Per-session breakdown is sorted by token rate descending
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/cross-session-aggregation.test.ts#Aggregate updates when a new session is added
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/cross-session-aggregation.test.ts#Aggregate updates when a session ends
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/cross-session-aggregation.test.ts#@property: aggregate total always equals sum of per-session values

steps.name: Context pressure tracker pure functions
steps.description: Add classifyContextUrgency and computeCompactionEstimate to performanceMonitor.ts. Urgency zones: normal (<70%), amber (70-89%), red (>=90%). Compaction estimate from remaining headroom and burn rate with confidence indicator.
criteria: Context below 70% classified as normal, at 70% as amber, at 90% as red
Boundary values 69%/70% and 89%/90% produce correct urgency transition
Compaction estimate computed from remaining tokens and burn rate
Low confidence when burn rate near zero
Threshold constants match Gauge Cluster values (shared config)

tests: tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/context-pressure.test.ts#User sees context utilization with urgency zones and compaction estimate
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/context-pressure.test.ts#Context below 70% shows normal urgency
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/context-pressure.test.ts#Context at 70% triggers amber urgency zone
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/context-pressure.test.ts#Context at 72% is within amber urgency zone
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/context-pressure.test.ts#Context at 90% triggers red urgency zone
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/context-pressure.test.ts#Context at 93% is deep in red urgency zone
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/context-pressure.test.ts#Context at 69% is the last normal level before amber
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/context-pressure.test.ts#Context at 89% is the last amber level before red
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/context-pressure.test.ts#Context at 100% is in red urgency zone
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/context-pressure.test.ts#Compaction estimate calculated from remaining headroom and burn rate
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/context-pressure.test.ts#Compaction estimate shows low confidence when burn rate near zero
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/context-pressure.test.ts#Context data unavailable produces safe default values
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/context-pressure.test.ts#@property: context urgency thresholds match Gauge Cluster thresholds

steps.name: Cost rate computation and aggregate view component
steps.description: Add computeCostRatePerMinute to performanceMonitor.ts. Build PMAggregateGrid.tsx (2x2 chart grid + breakdown panel) and PMChart.tsx (reusable Canvas chart cell reusing oscilloscope rendering functions).
criteria: Cost rate per minute computed from aggregate cost rate
Zero cost rate correctly displayed during idle periods
Aggregate grid renders tokens/s, cost/min, agents, and context charts
Per-session breakdown panel lists sessions with individual metrics

tests: tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/cross-session-aggregation.test.ts#User sees rolling cost rate across all active sessions
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/cross-session-aggregation.test.ts#Zero cost rate when all sessions are idle

steps.name: Navigation state machine
steps.description: Add discriminated union routing functions to performanceMonitor.ts: createAggregateViewMode, createSessionDetailViewMode, computeBreadcrumb, computeSessionDetailData. PMViewMode routes aggregate vs session-detail.
criteria: Aggregate view mode is the default navigation state
Session detail mode captures selected session identifier
Breadcrumb reflects current path: 'Performance Monitor' or 'PM > session'
Session detail data computable from frozen metrics after session ends

tests: tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/session-drill-down.test.ts#User drills into a session to investigate a token rate spike
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/session-drill-down.test.ts#Aggregate view mode is the default
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/session-drill-down.test.ts#Session detail view mode captures the selected session
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/session-drill-down.test.ts#Breadcrumb reflects current navigation path
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/session-drill-down.test.ts#Navigation back from detail to aggregate preserves time window
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/session-drill-down.test.ts#Session end during detail view produces frozen state indicator
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/session-drill-down.test.ts#Ended session removed from aggregate after back navigation

steps.name: Session detail view component
steps.description: Build PMSessionDetail.tsx: session-scoped charts, agent breakdown panel, operational metrics bar, back navigation. Handles graceful degradation when agent data absent and session-end indicator.
criteria: Session detail shows session-scoped charts and agent breakdown
Agent breakdown degrades gracefully when agent data absent
Session end during detail view shows frozen state indicator
Back button returns to aggregate view

tests: tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/session-drill-down.test.ts#Session detail includes agent breakdown when agent data is available
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/session-drill-down.test.ts#Session detail degrades gracefully when agent data is absent

steps.name: Multi-window sampler with downsampling
steps.description: Create multiWindowSampler.ts: TIME_WINDOW_PRESETS const array, createMultiWindowBuffer, appendMultiWindowSample, getActiveWindowSamples, computeMultiWindowStats, resolveSessionWindowConfig. Maintains ring buffers at 1m/5m/15m/session resolutions.
criteria: 1m preset: 600 samples at 100ms; 5m: 600 at 500ms; 15m: 900 at 1s
Session-length window computes dynamic resolution targeting 300-900 points
Buffer at capacity wraps correctly evicting oldest samples
Partially filled window produces valid stats from available data

tests: tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/time-window.test.ts#User switches from 1-minute to 15-minute window to see resource trend
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/time-window.test.ts#Time window presets have correct resolution and capacity
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/time-window.test.ts#Session-length window computes dynamic resolution for full history
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/time-window.test.ts#Stats computed from 5-minute window differ from 1-minute window
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/time-window.test.ts#Time window selection is independent of view mode
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/time-window.test.ts#1-minute window buffer at capacity wraps correctly
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/time-window.test.ts#Partially filled window still produces valid stats

steps.name: Time window selector UI and integration
steps.description: Build PMTimeWindowSelector.tsx: button group for 1m/5m/15m/Session. Wire multi-window sampler into multi-session store and PM view container. Time window state preserved across aggregate/detail navigation.
criteria: Time window selector offers 1m, 5m, 15m, and Session options
All charts respond to time window changes
Time window persists across aggregate/detail navigation
1-minute window preserves approximately 10Hz update feel

tests: tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/time-window.test.ts#Time window persists when switching from aggregate to session detail
tests/acceptance/norbert-performance-monitor/time-window.test.ts#Charts update when time window selection changes

validation.revision_notes: D1 (blocker): fixed 03-01 deps to include 02-02. D2 (high): added tests for 04-02. D6: fixed PMAggregatGrid typo. D3/D4/D5: accepted as advisory.
